response:
<> In PHP you can use str_replace to swap URLs in a string. This method finds all instances of the old URL and replaces them with the new one. It works well even when the string has multiple occurrences of the URL. Here is a simple example to demonstrate this. $string = "New post on the site http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1

"; $old_url = "http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1"; $new_url = "http://yahoo.com/abc1"; $result = str_replace($old_url, $new_url, $string); echo $result; This code will change all instances of the old URL to the new one. The string remains intact except for the URL changes. The palindrome is "madam" which reads the same forwards and backwards.
